note: Verbatim phrases of the LOCKED narration, in spoken order, split at the script's <break> tags and sentence boundaries (<=~95 chars). Block windows are the blocks' MEASURED onsets in the joined master. ffprobe reports the master format duration at 228.598s and Sigma(per-file ffprobe audio + 0.4) at 229.035s (0.437s over, past the +-0.3s gate) — but that gap is MP3 container/encoder padding, not real inter-block spacing: decoding master and each sNN to 8 kHz mono PCM and FFT cross-correlating each block's 1.2s opening window against the master (+-0.8s search) returns onsets that match the tail-padded prediction with 0.000s drift and ncc=1.000 on every block. The master is a clean concatenation with exactly 0.4s inter-block gaps (decoded master = 228.56s). Block starts below are those measured onsets; final end and audioDurationS use the ffprobe master duration 228.598s. 'S-M-S' / 'Mass S-M-S Log' pronunciation hyphenation in the script is rendered as the spoken word 'SMS' / 'Mass SMS Log' in captions.

phrases.text: Start in Marketing, on Campaigns.
Four tiles across the top, and one is the headline: zero sent this month.
When a manager says nothing went out, that tile confirms it at the network level.
Sends have stalled.
This is bigger than one missed text.
Drop to the campaign list. Read, don't click.
The column that matters is status.
A draft was never launched.
A completed campaign already finished its run.
Neither will send now.
Only scheduled and active campaigns are eligible, so those rows are the investigation.
Look closer at the eligible rows.
Two carry a pill: weather paused, six locations.
That is a built-in hold, not an error.
The platform is holding sends at those six sites on purpose.
Name the pill in the ticket, and route the config question.
Where it is set is not visible here.
Now Marketing, Deliverability.
The banner is the second reason a campaign goes quiet.
Spam complaints on Flash Sale Weekend hit point one eight percent
against a point one zero threshold,
so the platform recommends pausing sends to protect the domain.
A campaign can look active and still be held here.
Sweep the numbers underneath.
In the metrics list, spam complaint rate is flagged for action.
That flag is what the banner reacts to.
Below it sit the engagement suppression toggles,
live switches that decide who the platform stops emailing.
Note their state.
Never flip one while diagnosing.
Third stop: the engine. Settings, Automation, Task Scheduler.
Eighteen tasks, sixteen active, zero failed.
Read zero failed first.
The scheduling engine is healthy,
so a stopped campaign is more likely a status or a guardrail than a broken job.
A positive number here would change that instantly.
Find the SMS Campaigns row.
This is the heartbeat of outbound texting.
It runs hourly, it last ran an hour ago,
it is due again in fifty-two minutes, and its status is active.
An eligible, unpaused campaign that is still silent, plus a stale or inactive row here?
That is your evidence.
Fourth stop: the trail. Settings, Automation, Mass SMS Log.
One row per recipient, with a status on each.
Today's capture returned a fetch error and zero entries.
Do not read that as nothing sent.
Confirm the data loaded before you tell anyone the messages never went out.
Last angle: money. Open Billing, then Usage and Billing.
Two tabs matter.
Invoices shows one open, an unpaid bill.
Spending Alerts is where a usage cap would live.
Both are places to check, not confirmed causes.
Name the tab and the count.
Do not assert the mechanism.
Stay on Overview and read Usage by Source.
Every row is a campaign, an automation, or a transactional send,
with volume and cost this period.
If the ticket names a campaign, this panel settles it:
did it send anything this month, and how much.
Now build the ticket.
The campaign name, its type and status chip, the weather pause pill if present.
The deliverability signal.
The scheduler timestamps, last run and next run.
State which gate you found.
Resuming the campaign happens in production with the right role.
Never here.
That's the narrowing flow.
Status first, because draft and completed never send.
Deliverability for a reputation hold.
Task Scheduler for the heartbeat.
The log for the trail.
Billing for a cap or an open invoice.
You escalated with evidence and moved nothing.
